question_text: What is the definition of a moment in physics?

options.label: A
options.text: The amount of force applied to an object

options.label: B
options.text: The turning effect of a force about a pivot, calculated as force multiplied by perpendicular distance from the pivot

options.label: C
options.text: The speed at which an object rotates around a point

options.label: D
options.text: The total weight of an object

explanation: A moment is specifically the turning effect produced by a force. It depends on both how large the force is and how far away from the pivot it acts. The larger either of these values, the larger the moment.
